summary: A Go-based CLI tool orchestrated by GLM agents to systematically generate and manage test coverage for runner, schema, and reporting modules.
architecture: monolithic CLI application with internal modular packages
patterns: Test-Driven Development (high volume of _test.go files and agent-driven test generation)
concerns: Over-reliance on automated agent-generated tests (glm-agents directories) may lead to brittle or superficial test coverage
Scattered test files across multiple agent-generated directories make maintenance and consolidation difficult
Untracked and staged files in git status suggest incomplete or interrupted workflow
Potential test duplication between root test files and agent-generated test files
suggestions: Consolidate agent-generated tests into the main test files to reduce clutter and duplication
Review and validate the quality of automated test assertions in runner and schema packages
Commit or stash the pending changes to maintain a clean working tree
Focus on core logic in runner.go and MCP handlers rather than expanding test surface area
